title: myrsine tahuatensis
intro: Myrsine tahuatensis, also known as the Andean boxwood or boxwood tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating gastrointestinal problems, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of Myrsine tahuatensis are typically made from the leaves and bark, which are often steeped in hot water to make a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment.
